purpose: Classify current-capital ETF implementation paths and estimate minimum viable capital for nested micro-futures breadth without a return backtest.
owner_authorization: User explicitly requested execution of B2 on 2026-07-15.

allowed: official public broker terms and API documentation
official exchange contract specifications and public margin information
official ETF issuer pages and public quote or factsheet fields
read-only public pages and downloadable public reference files
data_rules.regional_capability_rule: Global Webull documentation is not evidence of Webull Thailand capability.
data_rules.unknown_rule: An unverified broker, fractional, margin, permission, currency, or API fact becomes a scope blocker and is never imputed as available.

etf_branch.implementation_style: US-listed fractional ETFs with global economic exposure; long or cash only

requirements: economic exposure matches one candidate sleeve
inception date and trading currency disclosed
fractional eligibility verified for the classified broker path
public fee and minimum-order terms available
tie_break_order: higher disclosed liquidity or trading volume

capital_and_trade_limits.trade_floor: maximum of USD 5, broker minimum, or 2 percent of current sleeve notional

cost_grid.commission: official broker schedule including applicable VAT or mandatory fees
cost_grid.funding_or_FX_cost: reported separately as one-time cost; unknown is a scope blocker

feasible_gate: every target trade meets the trade floor and verified broker minimum
cash buffer is at least 10 percent and gross exposure is at most 90 percent
no leverage or margin is required
maximum standalone risk contribution is at most 16 percent
maximum notional sleeve weight is at most 25 percent
primary stressed recurring cost is at most 1.5 percent of capital
fractional order capability is verified for the exact broker path

futures_branch.purpose: minimum-capital sizing study only; not a deployment path

contract_selection.micro_rule: smallest exchange-listed contract with public specifications and a plausible IBKR symbol for the market
contract_selection.full_size_rule: standard contract for the same root exposure where available
contract_selection.measurement_contract: nearest sufficiently liquid non-delivery contract shown by the official source at access time
contract_selection.unavailable_market_rule: retain the slot as a blocker; do not replace it after observing a favorable capital result

futures_branch.minimum_capital_formula: round up to the next USD 100 the maximum of initial_margin_sum/0.40, stressed_margin_sum/0.60, aggregate_stress_loss/0.30, and maximum_single_market_stress_loss/per_market_limit
futures_branch.operational_rule: Economic minimum capital does not prove IBKR permission, market-data entitlement, or order capability; unverified account capability is a separate scope blocker.

decision_rules.ETF_feasible: All ETF feasible gates pass for the named capital and broker path.
decision_rules.ETF_scope_restricted: Economic sizing passes but broker/API/fractional/currency capability remains unverified, or only a narrower manual path passes.
decision_rules.ETF_infeasible: Fewer than 8 sleeves or any locked capital, concentration, trade-floor, leverage, or cost gate fails.
decision_rules.futures_minimum_capital_only: A reproducible minimum capital is calculated but current capital or operational permission fails.
decision_rules.futures_infeasible: Required contract or margin/risk inputs are too incomplete to calculate the locked formula honestly.

report_requirements: hypothesis_id, evidence_tier E0, tier_blockers, edge_claim none
source inventory with access dates and regional-scope caveats
USD 1000 and USD 2000 ETF results by broker path
one-contract granularity, margin stress, cost, cash, and concentration diagnostics
explicit distinction between fact, locked assumption, calculation, and recommendation
hard_stops: post-result weakening of a locked threshold
